other_observers: Andy Boyce; The finder of the bird was Oswald, I don't know his first name. A few other birders that I know saw the bird is Brandon Percival and Mark Peterson.
specific_location: On the second pond back from the enterance of the Rocky Ford SWA. The pond was comma shaped.
description: It was a medium to small sized heron. It had a yellow head with two plumes coming off the back of the head. It also had a yellow eye. It had a black face, except fot a comma shapes check patch. The body was blueish gray, except for a small white patch on the upper front part of the wing. It had yellow legs. The gray bill was long and thick, but not as long as most herons.Behaviors: It was sitting in a pile of sticks that was in the water of a medium sized pond. It was standing there and didn't move, except its head the whole time we were watching the bird.Call: Didn't call or singPlumage: Adult
